What is the worst-case lookup time? What is the worst-case space usage?
A hash table is a data structure that uses a hash function to map keys to indices in an array. The hash function is used to convert the key into an index in the array, which is then used to store the value associated with the key. The hash function is designed to distribute the keys evenly across the array, so that the lookup time is O(1) on average. The worst-case lookup time is O(n), where n is the number of keys in the hash table. The worst-case space usage is O(n), where n is the number of keys in the hash table.
What is the difference between a hash table and a binary search tree?
A
